title: Fix 4 Browse Skill Bugs (Round 2)
overview: 4 independent bugs in the browse skill: text extractor mutates live DOM, tab snapshots lost on context recreation, scoped cursor-interactive selectors break when ancestor ID is outside scope, and mutation test gives false confidence.
scopeChallenge: All 4 bugs are genuine, independent, and small. No over-engineering needed — each is a 10-30 line fix in 1-2 files. REDUCTION mode selected.

failureModes.risk: TreeWalker misses text that innerText captured
failureModes.mitigation: Test on content-heavy sites after fix; fall back to textContent with post-processing if needed

failureModes.risk: Tab ID mapping edge case with 0 tabs before recreation
failureModes.mitigation: Handle empty tabUrls — no snapshots to migrate, just clear

failureModes.risk: Scope root has no matching elements for cursor scan
failureModes.mitigation: Already handled — findCursorInteractiveElements returns empty array

tasks.title: Fix text extraction to avoid DOM mutation
tasks.description: Replace the clone-append-to-body approach in read-commands.ts and meta-commands.ts with a TreeWalker-based extraction that never appends anything to the live DOM. Walk visible text nodes, skip script/style/noscript/svg and hidden elements.
acceptanceCriteria: browse text returns meaningful page content (no script/style text, preserves visible text)
browse diff url1 url2 still produces correct diffs
Running text on a page with childList:true MutationObserver does NOT increment mutation count

tasks.title: Migrate tab snapshots across context recreation
tasks.description: In browser-manager.ts recreateContext(), build an old→new tab ID map during the tab recreation loop, then remap tabSnapshots keys. Save/restore tabSnapshots on rollback.
acceptanceCriteria: After emulate device, snapshot-diff still has the previous baseline and produces a diff
After a failed recreateContext() rollback, tabSnapshots is restored to pre-attempt state
Multi-tab scenario: each tab retains its own snapshot baseline after context recreation

tasks.title: Scope-aware ancestor ID lookup for cursor-interactive selectors
tasks.description: In snapshot.ts findCursorInteractiveElements(), pass scopeSelector into page.evaluate(). When building the ancestor ID anchor, check if the ancestor is inside the scope root — if not, skip it. Also recompute selectorIndex against the scope root when scoped.
acceptanceCriteria: snapshot -C -s [id=main] only produces refs with selectors that resolve correctly via page.locator(scope).locator(cssSelector)
When ancestor ID is outside scope, selector falls back to element-only and still resolves
Unscoped -C continues to work as before

tasks.title: Fix mutation observer test to detect childList mutations
tasks.description: Enable childList: true in mutation-observer.html so the test actually detects DOM append/remove. After TASK-001 fixes the text extractor, this test should pass (count stays 0).
acceptanceCriteria: mutation-observer.html observer config has childList: true
After TASK-001 fix, bun test mutation observer test passes (count remains 0)
Test would fail if the old clone-append approach were still used
